text.title: code-coverage Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for coverage as a CI gate.

rule.statement: Use branch coverage (`--cov-branch` / `--branches`) as the CI gate metric. Line coverage misses uncovered `else` branches on covered `if` lines.
rule.rationale: A test exercising the if-True path covers all if-line tokens, but the else branch is untested. Branch coverage requires both sides to execute.

rule.statement: Gate diff coverage (changed lines) at 90% via `diff-cover --fail-under=90 --compare-branch=origin/main`. Set repo total as a soft trend target (~80%), not a hard gate.
rule.rationale: Hard gates on repo total block PRs on codebases with legacy untested code. Diff gating locks new code without forcing full uplift at once.

rule.statement: Do NOT chase 100% repo coverage. Set 80-90% as the practical ceiling. The last 10-20% is defensive guards / error paths better validated by integration tests.
rule.rationale: Chasing 100% leads to testing trivial getters, mocking everything, and writing assertions that mirror implementation rather than verify behaviour.

rule.statement: Every CI run publishes `coverage.xml` as a PR artifact. PR comment summarises diff coverage % and the 3 largest uncovered branches.
rule.rationale: Reviewers cannot enforce coverage without seeing it. Artifact + summary comment makes the gate visible in PR review.

rule.statement: On modules tagged `critical` (auth, payments, RBAC), run mutation testing (mutmut / Stryker / cosmic-ray) weekly. Surviving mutants fail the job.
rule.rationale: Coverage % can be high while assertions are weak. Mutation testing kills the tests that pass even when the production code is wrong.
